deck.author: OpenAI Codex
deck.subject: Demonstration presentation
deck.title: Test presentation
deck.outline:
1. Test presentation - System Initialization: Test Presentation Protocol
2. Test Parameters & Scope - Establishing the baseline for functional verification.
3. Core Logic Verification - Demonstrating core logic and data processing capabilities.
4. Stress Testing & Edge Cases - Evaluating system performance under stress and boundary conditions.
5. Evaluation Handover - Protocol complete: Transitioning to user evaluation.

slide-01.intent: Introduce the testing protocol and objectives.
slide-01.layoutHint: High-contrast technical header with minimalist geometric background.
slide-01.notes: This slide serves as the initial entry point to set the technical context and define the scope of the verification process.
slide-01.title: Test presentation

slide-02.layoutHint: Structured list or table format with high legibility.
slide-02.mustInclude: Establishing the baseline for functional verification.
slide-02.notes: This slide establishes the baseline for functional verification by defining what is included and excluded from the test environment.
slide-02.title: Test Parameters & Scope

slide-03.layoutHint: Monospaced font for technical accuracy; dark background with light text.
slide-03.mustInclude: Demonstrating core logic and data processing capabilities.
slide-03.notes: This slide serves as the execution phase for verifying core processing logic and data handling capabilities.
slide-03.title: Core Logic Verification

slide-04.layoutHint: Comparison charts showing nominal vs. failure states.
slide-04.mustInclude: Evaluating system performance under stress and boundary conditions.
slide-04.notes: This slide analyzes the trade-offs between system stability and performance under extreme conditions.
slide-04.title: Stress Testing & Edge Cases

slide-05.intent: Summarize findings and transition to user feedback loop.
slide-05.mustInclude: Protocol complete: Transitioning to user evaluation.
slide-05.notes: This slide marks the end of the automated testing sequence and initiates the manual user review process.
slide-05.title: Evaluation Handover
